input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.7.6.1 specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.7.6.1
            TITLE: when forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.6.1: When forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles:
T.7.6.1 When forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles:   
Inboard of two vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the outside of 
the front tires at the height of the hubs.

intermediate_steps.founded_objects: dimension arrow, dimension line, axis line

final_answer:
Explanation: Rule T.7.6.1 mandates that the vehicle structure forward of the front wheel axles must be inboard of two vertical planes defined by the outside edges of the front tires at the hub height. The engineering drawing provides an overall width dimension of 193.31 mm for the front section. However, the drawing does not explicitly provide the dimensions of the front tires or the required maximum width based on the tires, which are necessary to verify compliance with this specific rule. As per instructions, if a dimension required for verification is not explicitly shown, compliance is assumed.

Answer: Yes
